mission.title: argo-rollouts: Support having multiple VirtualService objects
mission.description: Support having multiple VirtualService objects. Requested by 29+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-rollouts deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-rollouts version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
helm list -n argo-rollouts 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-rollouts installation.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
Rollout does not seem to support having (specifically, updating weights in) multiple VirtualService objects.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support having multiple VirtualService objects
steps.description:
Currently, Argo Rollout spec only supports a single VirtualService (VS) in its Istio integrated canary.
Multiple VirtualService support is needed in Istio-based setups in which a service's routes are given in multiple VirtualService objects rather than just a single one.

Use cases:
This feature is
```yaml
# Istio traffic routing configuration
        istio:
          virtualService: 
            name: rollout-vsvc  # required
            routes:
            - primary # At least one route is required
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support having multiple VirtualService objects" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Currently, Argo Rollout spec only supports a single VirtualService (VS) in its Istio integrated canary.
Multiple VirtualService support is needed in Istio-based setups in which a service's routes are given in multiple VirtualService objects rather than just a single one.
codeSnippets:
# Istio traffic routing configuration
        istio:
          virtualService: 
            name: rollout-vsvc  # required
            routes:
            - primary # At least one route is required

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
